subject: Project Management
title: Scrum in Hardware
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Scrum in Hardware.

hook.text: Scrum was built for software. Can it work for physical products you can't undo with a keystroke?

buildup.text: Hardware teams at John Deere and Saab defense started running sprints with physical prototypes.

discovery.text: They built rough models each sprint — 3D prints, cardboard mockups, breadboard circuits.

twist.text: Hardware sprints are harder — you can't 'refactor' a jet engine. But feedback loops still help.

climax.text: Scrum in hardware proves the framework's core idea: iterate, learn, adapt — regardless of material.

punchline.text: If it works for jet engines, it works for anything.

quiz.question: How do hardware teams apply Scrum?
options: By building rough physical prototypes each sprint
